description: You are a traffic signal engineer at the Municipality of Bologna's Mobility Department (Settore Mobilita). The Acosta corridor experiences severe congestion due to poorly coordinated traffic signals along the main arterial route. Your task is to implement a 'green wave' (onda verde) by optimizing signal offsets along the corridor so that vehicles traveling at the design speed (50 km/h) encounter consecutive green lights. Using the Bologna Acosta scenario at /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/, evaluate the baseline performance and save the metrics (avg_travel_time_s, avg_waiting_time_s, avg_time_loss_s, total_vehicles) to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/baseline_corridor_metrics.csv using a metric,value column format. Create a coordinated traffic light signal (TLS) additional file at /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/acosta_tls_coordinated.add.xml by adjusting offsets for at least 4 of the 7 signalized intersections (TLS IDs: 209, 210, 219, 220, 221, 235, 273) based on inter-intersection distances. Configure and run a modified simulation using /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/run_coordinated.sumocfg and save the resulting performance metrics to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/coordinated_corridor_metrics.csv. Produce a coordination assessment report at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/signal_coordination_report.txt documenting the chosen offset values and rationale, a before/after comparison of corridor metrics, percentage improvements achieved, and professional recommendations for implementation.
